title: Class of 2030 Move In

text:
Class of 2030 Move In
Move Into Jingyao Residence Hall
Move-In Day: Sunday, August 23, 2026
Move-In Day is one of the most highly anticipated days of the university experience. After spending an entire summer preparing for your journey to NYU Shanghai, you will finally arrive to pick up your campus card, and open the door to your new home.
All NYU Shanghai first-year students live with someone of a different nationality, participating in an exchange of knowledge, culture, and ideas. To help you navigate this busy time, we will provide you with a packing list of suggested items in one of the summer newsletters, and give you more details about your housing assignment.
NYU Shanghai will start the fall semester on Monday, August 31, 2026. Students will move into their residence hall on Sunday, August 23, 2026, and join a week-long orientation before classes start.
Welcome Tables
Residence Hall: Our staff, Residence Assistants, and Orientation Ambassadors will be on site to welcome you from 9 AM to 9 PM on Move-In Day.
Campus: Our staff and Orientation Ambassadors will be welcoming you from 9 AM to 5:30 PM on campus on Move-in Day. You will be able to pick up your Welcome Bag, class t-shirt, set up your phone and bank, and receive guidance on starting your journey at NYU Shanghai.
NYU Shanghai Airport Pickup Service & Arrival Transportation
On Move-In Day, August 23, 2026, NYU Shanghai will arrange airport pickup services to help students get to the residence hall from the Pudong International Airport. Detailed arrangements will be announced in one of the summer newsletters.
If you are not able to take the airport shuttle buses, you can refer to the information below to arrange your own transportation to the residence hall.
Arrival Transportation
Airports
There are two airports in Shanghai:
Shanghai Pudong International Airport (PVG)
Shanghai Hongqiao International Airport (SHA)
The two airports are connected by Airport Link Line or Metro Line 2. Taxi rides to the Jingyao Residence Halls from Pudong Airport takes about 40 mins, and around 150 RMB. From Hongqiao Airport it takes about 30 mins, and around 60 RMB.
Railway Stations
There are three main railway stations in Shanghai:
Shanghai Hongqiao Railway Station
Shanghai Railway Station
Shanghai South Railway Station
The majority of the trains stop at the Hongqiao Railway Station.
Metro & Maglev
Metro line 2 runs from both of Shanghai's international airports and it can transfer at Longyang Rd station for Line 7 to arrive at Houtan station, which is a 10-min walk away from the new residence hall. It will cost about 10 RMB.
From Pudong Airport: Take the Maglev to Longyang Rd (it only takes about 5 minutes), and then transfer to Line 7 . Then get off at Houtan Station.
From Hongqiao Airport: Take line 2 then transfer to line 7 at Jing'an Temple Station. Then get off at Houtan Station.
When planning your arrival, please be aware that it may be inconvenient to carry large amounts of luggage with you on the metro. Also be mindful of the earliest and latest running time of the Metro is normally 6AM - 10PM. Maglev trains stop operation even earlier.
Taxi
Taking a taxi is the most convenient way to get from the airport to the residence hall or campus. When you enter the arrival hall after picking up your luggages, look for signs leading you to the taxi queue. Make sure you have the address of your destination ready in Chinese, since most taxi drivers do not speak English.
The fare to the residence hall usually costs around 60 RMB (around 10 USD) from Hongqiao Airport, and it costs around 150 RMB (22 USD) from Pudong Airport depending on traffic at your time of arrival. The rate will increase between 11 PM and 5 AM. More advice about using taxis can be found in the NYU Shanghai Online Pre-Arrival Orientation, which will be emailed to you during the summer.
